<hr style="height:0pt; visibility:hidden;" />

# ruff

Modern, ultra-fast linter that implements checks from Flake8 and some other popular plugins.

lintquarto uses ruff as a read-only linter by running `ruff check` on a temporary Python version of your `.qmd` files. It does not support extra commands from ruff like `ruff check --fix` (which automatically applies some fixes) or `ruff format` (a code formatter). This means the Ruff checks you see in lintquarto will not cover every issue that could be fixed by the formatter, because Ruff's linter is [intentionally more restricted](https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff/issues/8232) in scope than its formatter and several stylistic issues are handled silently by the formatter rather than reported as lint errors.

Supporting automatic fixes for `.qmd` files ([lintquarto issue #93](https://github.com/lintquarto/lintquarto/issues/93)) would require safely mapping Ruff's edits on the temporary `.py` file back into the original `.qmd` source, which is non-trivial, so lintquarto currently focuses on reporting issues without modifying your Quarto documents.

```{python}
#| echo: false
# Import required packages
import subprocess
import sys

# Add parent directory to sys.path
sys.path.insert(0, "..")

# Import the helper functions
from helpers import generate_html, print_quarto
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
generate_html(
    pypi_url="https://pypi.org/project/ruff/",
    github_url="https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff",
    conda_url="https://anaconda.org/conda-forge/ruff"
)
```

## Example Quarto `.qmd` file:

{{< downloadthis examples/general_example.qmd dname="general_example" label="Download the example" type="light" >}}

:::{.pale-grey}
```{python}
#| echo: false
print_quarto("examples/general_example.qmd")
```
:::

## Run ruff using `lintquarto`

```{.bash}
lintquarto -l ruff -p general_example.qmd
```

:::{.pale-green}
<style>
.cell-output-stdout code {
  word-break: break-word !important;
  white-space: pre-wrap !important;
}
</style>
```{python}
#| echo: false
result = subprocess.run(
    ["lintquarto", "-l", "ruff", "-p", "examples/general_example.qmd"],
    capture_output=True, text=True, check=False
)
print(result.stdout)
```
:::
